items.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
items.summary: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

items.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind
items.summary: The invisible force behind all of this is no mystery to anyone paying attention to the city's tech economy. San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

items.title: Prime Video follows Netflix and Disney by adding a TikTok-like ‘Clips’ feed in its app
items.summary: The Clips feed aims to enable discovery by offering users a scrollable feed with short snippets of shows and movies.

items.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
items.summary: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

items.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
items.summary: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

items.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
items.summary: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. items.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
items.summary: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

items.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
items.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.

items.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
items.summary: A report by Poland’s top intelligence agency accused Russia of sabotage and hacking activities against the country’s military and civilian infrastructure.

items.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
items.summary: Former cybersecurity executive Peter Williams stole several surveillance and hacking tools and sold them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker that works with Putin’s government.

items.title: Pentagon releases UFO files on new website
items.summary: The initial set of files housed on the site will include those containing unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) videos, photos, and original source documents from across the entire U.S. government. items.title: Truecaller slashes 70 jobs amid declining ad sales
items.summary: Truecaller's ad revenue declined by 44%, leading to the layoffs.

items.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
items.summary: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

items.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
items.summary: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

items.title: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company, files for IPO
items.summary: After years of hints and preparation, Lime plans to enter the public markets. The company intends to list on ​Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LIME." Lime did not share terms of the offering.

items.title: The biggest US power grid is under strain from AI — and no one is happy
items.summary: PJM Interconnection — which oversees the grid for some of the densest data center developments on Earth — wants to overhaul itself. Not everyone thinks it's up to the task.

items.title: Airbnb says AI now writes 60% of its new code
items.summary: Airbnb also said its customer support AI bot now handles 40% of issues without escalating to a human agent.

items.title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
items.summary: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. items.title: Trump reportedly plans to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
items.summary: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

items.title: ABC refuses to capitulate to Trump admin, fights FCC probe into The View
items.summary: FCC chair hasn't been able to bully ABC and owner Disney into submission.
